name: Chicken Big Mamou on Pasta
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Fresh spaghetti (equivalent to 1 pound dry)
recipeInstructions:
-------------
SEASONING MIX
2 teaspoons Dried thyme leaves
3/4 teaspoon Black pepper
1 1/4 teaspoons Ground cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon Dried sweet basil leaves
1 teaspoon White pepper
SAUCE
1 pound Unsalted butter plus 2 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce
2 pounds Unsalted butter
1 tablespoon Tabasco sauce + 1 teaspoon
1 cup Onions, chopped very fine
2 cups Tomato sauce (16 oz)
4 medium garlic cloves, peeled
2 tablespoons Minced garlic
2 teaspoons Chopped green onions
RICH CHICKEN STOCK
1 3/4 cups Rich chicken stock (see note)
CHICKEN SEASONING MIX
1 1/2 tablespoons Salt
1 teaspoon Black pepper
1 tablespoon White pepper
1 teaspoon Cumin (optional)
1 tablespoon Garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon Dried sweet basil leaves
1 1/4 teaspoons Ground cayenne pepper
2 pounds Boneless chicken
NOTE: To make a Rich Chicken Stock, strain the basic stock and continue simmering until the liquid reduces by half. For example, if you need 1 cup of rich stock, start with at least 2 cups of strained basic stock.
----------
Place hot water, oil, and salt in a large pot over high heat; cover and bring to a boil. When water reaches a rolling boil, add small amounts of spaghetti at a time, breaking up any oil patches as you drop the spaghetti into the pot. Return to boiling and cook until al dente, ensuring not to overcook (To test doneness, cut a strand near the end of cooking; when done, there should be only a speck of white in the center, less than one-fourth the diameter of the strand). Do not overcook. Use a wooden or spaghetti spoon to lift spaghetti out by spoonfuls and shake strands back into boiling water during this time. After cooking, immediately drain spaghetti through a colander; stop the cooking process with cold water (if using dry pasta, first rinse with hot water to remove starch). Allow pasta to cool for about 2 to 3 minutes, then toss with vegetable oil in your hands and set aside.
Meanwhile, combine seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside. In a 4-quart saucepan, sauté butter, onions, and garlic over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add minced garlic and seasoning mix; continue cooking until onions are dark brown but not burned (about 8 to 10 minutes), stirring often. Add the remaining stock, Worcestershire sauce, and Tabasco; bring to a fast simmer and cook about 8 minutes, stirring frequently. Stir in tomato sauce and bring mixture to a boil. Then stir in sugar and green onions; gently simmer uncovered for about 40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Heat serving plates in an oven set at 250°F. Combine chicken seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and mix well. Sprinkle over the chicken, rubbing it into the meat with your hands. In a large skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add remaining green onions and sauté for about 3 minutes. Add chicken and continue cooking for 10 minutes, stirring frequently. When tomato sauce has simmered for about 40 minutes, stir in the chicken mixture and heat through.
To serve, melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add one-sixth of the cooked spaghetti (less than a cup measure) and heat for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Stir in chicken and sauce, along with remaining stock; heat thoroughly by stirring frequently. Remove from heat. Using a fork, roll spaghetti onto a heated serving plate. Repeat process for remaining servings.
